byHayden Donaldson

QUINCY, Ill. (KHQA) —Local law enforcement showed their support of local  athletes by participating in the annual Torch Run for the Special Olympics.

Officers from the Quincy Police Department and Adams County Sheriff's Office ran about 1.5 miles from Saint Dominic's School to Transitions of Western Illinois.

Event organizer Tara Nickerson says that support for Special Olympics athletes should never go away.

"A lot of times people with intellectual disabilities are disregarded," Nickerson said. "They're viewed as just on the sidelines, and that's not the way they ought to feel because every human being deserves to be celebrated, and that's what Special Olympics is about."

The Special Olympics has raised over $2 million this year for the upcoming summer games.

The Illinois Special Olympics summer games will be this weekend, June 7-9th, at Hancock Stadium in Bloomington-Normal.

2025Sinclair, Inc.
title: Running for a cause: Local law enforcement run for Special Olympics Illinois
description: Local law enforcement showed their support of local athletes by participating in the annual Torch Run for the Special Olympics.
